artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found an ECNU central news page that republishes a student AIGC guide explicitly scoped to ECNU Communication College and Beijing Normal University Journalism and Communication students, plus ECNU's graduate academic integrity regulation. No central university-wide binding generative-AI policy was identified in the searched official sources.

artifacts.claimText: ECNU's published Communication College student AIGC guide says students may use AIGC in accordance with academic integrity, but direct AIGC-generated content should be separated, marked, and kept within 20% of the full text.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 学生需要将AIGC直接生成的内容与学生个人的学术贡献分开，并明确标注AIGC在学生学业相关任务中的贡献。对AIGC直接生成内容的部分需标红，直接生成的内容不能超过全文的20%。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students should separate and mark direct AIGC-generated content, and direct generated content should not exceed 20% of the full text.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 3, 使用AIGC时应进行标注

artifacts.title: East China Normal University staged AI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Official ECNU-domain discovery found a central ECNU page republishing a student generative-AI use guide that is explicitly scoped to ECNU Communication College and Beijing Normal University Journalism and Communication students. The staged AI claims therefore describe college-scoped guidance on marking direct AIGC content, a 20% direct-generation limit, self-checking output, and following course requirements. No central university-wide binding AI policy was identified from the searched official sources. ECNU's graduate academic integrity regulation is included separately as non-AI-specific academic-integrity context.
limitations: This staged run found no central university-wide binding generative-AI policy for East China Normal University; AI-specific claims are scoped to the cited Communication College guide.
Rejected official sources included research and event/news pages because they did not provide durable policy or guidance language.
This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
